title: Market Brief — Tuesday, April 28, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at a severe 11% (77/308), H200 at 23% (40/306), and B200 at 17% (138/408) — none clearing the 25% threshold. The headline move: H200 spot crashed 32.4% day-over-day to $2.33/hr despite tight supply, signaling either a demand shock or inventory dump worth watching closely.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at a severe 11% (77/308), H200 at 23% (40/306), and B200 at 17% (138/408) — none clearing the 25% threshold. The headline move: H200 spot crashed 32.4% day-over-day to $2.33/hr despite tight supply, signaling either a demand shock or inventory dump worth watching closely.

At 1.25x TCO, H100 true cost runs $7.16/M tokens on Llama 70B, making medium-tier pricing a cash furnace at negative 91% margin. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output is selling at a loss of roughly $2.27/M tokens on H100 infrastructure — textbook market-share buying. Provider grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash, Meta Burning Cash.

B200 spot at $4.00/hr is overvalued given 56.7% annualized volatility and thinning 17% availability that could snap either direction. H200 at $2.33/hr post-crash is undervalued against its OCPI fair value of $3.15/hr — buy H200 spot capacity now and arbitrage the 35% discount before availability tightens further.
bullets: 11% H100 availability means only 77 of 308 GPUs are free, a severe shortage.
40 of 306 H200 GPUs available at 23%, still below the scarcity threshold of 25%.
$2.33 H200 spot median sits 26% below its $3.15 OCPI trade-based fair value.
21.41 LLMflation index means token prices have fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.378 per million output tokens makes DeepSeek V3.2 the cheapest frontier-adjacent model tracked.
42.2 percentage points of B200 utilization gain in 30 days signals surging next-gen inference demand.
